name: Taj Mahal
description: The Taj Mahal was commissioned in 1632 by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan as a memorial to his wife Mumtaz Mahal.
Its marble surfaces, gardens, and balanced geometry make it one of the most recognizable monuments in the world.
The site works well for TravelVerse because architecture, emotion, and royal history come together in a single visitor story.

name: Qutub Minar
description: Qutub Minar is a monumental tower from the Delhi Sultanate, built across multiple reigns from the late 12th century onward.
Its carved sandstone bands and inscriptions make it a strong visual example of layered political and architectural history.
TravelVerse can use this site to explain dynasties, design evolution, and the wider Qutub complex in one flow.

name: Charminar
description: Charminar was built in 1591 and remains the symbolic heart of Hyderabad.
Its four minarets and surrounding market streets connect architecture with the living rhythm of the old city.
This makes it a strong TravelVerse story because heritage, faith, trade, and culture all intersect in one landmark.
